name: Effect v4 Correction: Context.GenericTag does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Context.GenericTag does not exist
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: import * as Context from "effect/Context"; const FsUtils = Context.GenericTag<FsUtils>("@beep/repo-utils/FsUtils")

Correct v4 Pattern: import { ServiceMap } from "effect"; const FsUtils = ServiceMap.Service<FsUtils>("@beep/repo-utils/FsUtils"); or class-based: class FsUtils extends ServiceMap.Service<FsUtils, FsUtilsShape>()("@beep/repo-utils/FsUtils", { make: Effect.gen(function*() { ... }) }) {}

Explanation: The entire Context module is gone from Effect v4. It is not exported from the barrel index.ts. Use ServiceMap.Service instead.

Affected Modules: effect/Context, effect/ServiceMap
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: @effect/platform imports moved to effect package
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: @effect/platform imports moved to effect package
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: import * as FileSystem from "@effect/platform/FileSystem"; import * as Path from "@effect/platform/Path"

Correct v4 Pattern: import { FileSystem, Path, PlatformError } from "effect"; // Service access: const fs = yield* FileSystem.FileSystem; const path = yield* Path.Path; // Node layers still from: import { NodeFileSystem, NodePath } from "@effect/platform-node"

Explanation: FileSystem and Path abstractions moved to the main effect package in v4. Only the Node.js implementations remain in @effect/platform-node. You may not need @effect/platform as a dependency at all.

Affected Modules: @effect/platform, effect/FileSystem, effect/Path, @effect/platform-node
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Schema.decode does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Schema.decode does not exist
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: const result = Schema.decode(PackageJson)(jsonData)

Correct v4 Pattern: Schema.decodeUnknownEffect(PackageJson)(data) for effectful decoding (returns Effect<Type, SchemaError>), Schema.decodeUnknownSync(PackageJson)(data) for synchronous decoding (throws on error), Schema.decodeSync(PackageJson)(data) for decoding from encoded type (not unknown).

Explanation: Schema.decode does not exist in v4. Use the explicit effectful or sync variants instead.

Affected Modules: effect/Schema
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: HashMap/HashSet toIterable not needed
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: HashMap.toIterable / HashSet.toIterable do not exist
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: for (const [key, value] of HashMap.toIterable(myMap)) { ... }

Correct v4 Pattern: for (const [key, value] of myMap) { ... } // or explicitly: for (const [key, value] of HashMap.entries(myMap)) { ... }

Explanation: Both HashMap and HashSet implement the Iterable interface directly in v4. No .toIterable() method is needed.

Affected Modules: effect/HashMap, effect/HashSet
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Layer.scoped does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Layer.scoped does not exist
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: const myLayer = Layer.scoped(MyService, scopedEffect)

Correct v4 Pattern: const myLayer = Layer.effect(MyService, effectfulConstruction); Layer.succeed(MyService, staticValue) for static values; Layer.sync(MyService, () => computedValue) for lazy values.

Explanation: Layer.effect already handles scoped resources and excludes Scope from the requirements type. No separate Layer.scoped is needed.

Affected Modules: effect/Layer
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 New Feature: Built-in Graph module
episode_body:
Title: v4 New Feature: Built-in Graph module with algorithms
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Effect v4 includes a built-in Graph module eliminating the need to implement graph algorithms from scratch.

Available APIs: import { Graph } from "effect"; Graph.directed<string, string>() and Graph.undirected<string, string>() for creation; Graph.addNode(graph, "node1"); Graph.addEdge(graph, "node1", "node2") for building; Graph.topo(graph) for topological sort (Kahn's algorithm); Graph.isAcyclic(graph) for cycle detection; Graph.dfs(graph, "start") for depth-first search; Graph.bfs(graph, "start") for breadth-first search; Graph.dfsPostOrder(graph) for DFS post-order traversal.

Affected Modules: effect/Graph
source_description: Effect v4 new capability from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 New Feature: Effect.provide accepts arrays of Layers
episode_body:
Title: v4 New Feature: Effect.provide accepts arrays of Layers
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Effect v4 allows providing multiple layers at once using an array: const program = myEffect.pipe(Effect.provide([LayerA, LayerB, LayerC]))

This is a convenience feature that eliminates the need to chain Layer.merge or similar combinators when providing multiple independent layers.

Affected Modules: effect/Effect
source_description: Effect v4 new capability from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Effect.catchAll does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Effect.catchAll does not exist
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: Effect.catchAll(effect, handler)

Correct v4 Pattern: Use Effect.catch or Effect.orElseSucceed instead. For catching specific errors by tag, use Effect.catchTag. For catching all errors and converting to an exit, use Effect.exit(effect).

Explanation: Effect.catchAll was removed in v4. The replacement depends on the specific use case.

Affected Modules: effect/Effect
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Str.endsWith is curried-only
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Str.endsWith is curried-only
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: Str.endsWith(str, suffix)

Correct v4 Pattern: Str.endsWith(suffix)(str) - the function is curried-only and does not support data-first style.

Explanation: In Effect v4, String module functions like endsWith use curried-only signatures.

Affected Modules: effect/String
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Order.String uses capital S
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Order.String uses capital S
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: Order.string

Correct v4 Pattern: Order.String (capital S)

Explanation: In Effect v4, the string ordering instance is accessed as Order.String, not Order.string.

Affected Modules: effect/Order
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: A.isArrayEmpty not A.isEmptyReadonlyArray
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: A.isArrayEmpty replaces A.isEmptyReadonlyArray
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: A.isEmptyReadonlyArray(arr)

Correct v4 Pattern: A.isArrayEmpty(arr)

Explanation: The array emptiness check was renamed in v4 from isEmptyReadonlyArray to isArrayEmpty.

Affected Modules: effect/Array
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: A.filterMap does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: A.filterMap does not exist
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: A.filterMap(arr, fn)

Correct v4 Pattern: Use A.getSomes with A.map returning Option: pipe(arr, A.map(item => someCondition ? O.some(transform(item)) : O.none()), A.getSomes)

Explanation: A.filterMap was removed in v4. The replacement pattern is to map items to Options and then use A.getSomes to extract the Some values.

Affected Modules: effect/Array
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Schema.Record takes two positional args
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: S.Record(KeySchema, ValueSchema) takes two positional args
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Correct v4 Pattern: S.Record(S.String, S.Number) - Schema.Record takes two positional arguments: the key schema and the value schema.

Explanation: This is a valid v4 syntax for creating record schemas with typed keys and values.

Affected Modules: effect/Schema
source_description: Effect v4 API pattern from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Layer.mock for test mocking
episode_body:
Title: v4 Pattern: Layer.mock(Service)({}) for test mocking
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Correct v4 Pattern: Layer.mock(Service)({}) creates a mock layer for testing purposes.

Explanation: In Effect v4, Layer.mock provides a convenient way to create mock service implementations for testing.

Affected Modules: effect/Layer
source_description: Effect v4 API pattern from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Schema.optionalKey vs Schema.optional
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Schema.optionalKey vs Schema.optional
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Correct v4 Pattern: Use Schema.optionalKey for key?: T (property may be missing entirely). Use Schema.optional for key?: T | undefined (property exists but may be undefined).

Explanation: v4 distinguishes between missing keys and undefined values in struct schemas.

Affected Modules: effect/Schema
source_description: Effect v4 API pattern from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: CLI is built-in at effect/unstable/cli
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: @effect/cli does not exist - use effect/unstable/cli
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: import { Command, Flag, Argument } from "@effect/cli"

Correct v4 Pattern: import { Command, Flag, Argument } from "effect/unstable/cli"

Explanation: @effect/cli does not exist as a standalone package in v4. The CLI module is built into the main effect package as an unstable module (~10K line module). Command.Environment provides FileSystem, Path, Terminal, and ChildProcessSpawner services.

Affected Modules: effect/unstable/cli
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Use Effect.void instead of Effect.succeed(undefined)
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Use Effect.void instead of Effect.succeed(void 0)
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: Effect.succeed(void 0) or Effect.succeed(undefined)

Correct v4 Pattern: Effect.void

Explanation: Effect.void is the idiomatic way to create an Effect that succeeds with undefined/void. The LSP warns about effectSucceedWithVoid.

Affected Modules: effect/Effect
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Pattern: Effect.fn for functions returning Effects
episode_body:
Title: v4 Pattern: Use Effect.fn instead of arrow functions returning Effect.gen
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: const foo = (a: T) => Effect.gen(function* () { ... })

Correct v4 Pattern: const foo: (a: T) => Effect.Effect<A, E, R> = Effect.fn(function* (a) { ... })

Explanation: Effect.fn is the preferred way to define functions that return Effects. This also applies to Command handlers and error callbacks.

Affected Modules: effect/Effect
source_description: Effect v4 coding pattern from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Pattern: A.getSomes, A.isArrayNonEmpty, A.unprepend, A.get
episode_body:
Title: v4 Pattern: New Array utility functions
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

New v4 Array utilities:
- A.getSomes filters Iterable<Option<A>> to Array<A> (replaces filterMap pattern)
- A.isArrayNonEmpty narrows Array<A> to NonEmptyArray<A> (mutable only)
- A.unprepend destructures NonEmptyArray: [head, tail]
- A.get(arr, i) returns Option<A> for safe indexed access

Affected Modules: effect/Array
source_description: Effect v4 new Array utilities from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Pattern: Use Str.match from effect/String instead of native .match
episode_body:
Title: v4 Pattern: Use Str.match instead of native .match(regex)
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: str.match(regex) - native .match returns nullable

Correct v4 Pattern: Str.match(regex) from effect/String - returns Option instead of nullable result

Explanation: Never use native .match(regex). Str.match(regex) wraps the result in an Option for type-safe pattern matching.

Affected Modules: effect/String
source_description: Effect v4 coding pattern from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Pattern: Use S.TaggedErrorClass instead of Data.TaggedError
episode_body:
Title: v4 Pattern: Always use S.TaggedErrorClass, never Data.TaggedError
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Incorrect Pattern: class MyError extends Data.TaggedError("MyError")<{ message: string }> {}

Correct v4 Pattern: export class MyError extends S.TaggedErrorClass<MyError>("@scope/pkg/path/to/module/SchemaName")("MyError", { message: S.String }, { title: "Human Readable Title", description: "Thoughtful description" }) {}

Explanation: Always use S.TaggedErrorClass from effect/Schema for defining tagged errors. First string arg is the schema identifier (module path + schema name). Second call takes: tag name, field schemas, and metadata (title + description).

Affected Modules: effect/Schema, effect/Data
source_description: Effect v4 coding pattern from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Blog: 4.0 Beta Release Announcement
episode_body:
Title: Effect 4.0 Beta Release Announcement
Category: concept
Source: effect.website blog

Key Changes:
- Complete fiber runtime rebuild for reduced memory overhead and faster execution
- Bundle size reduction from ~70kB (v3) to ~20kB (v4) for Effect+Stream+Schema
- Unified versioning: all Effect packages share one version number instead of independent versioning
- Package consolidation: @effect/platform, @effect/rpc, @effect/cluster integrated into core effect package
- Unstable modules via effect/unstable/* import paths (17 modules covering AI, HTTP, Schema, SQL, RPC, CLI, workflows, clustering)

Motivation: Simplify dependency management, reduce bundle sizes, improve runtime performance, and provide a coherent ecosystem where all packages move in lockstep.

Highlights:
- Core programming model (Effect, Layer, Schema, Stream) remains unchanged
- Remaining specialized packages: @effect/platform-*, @effect/sql-*, @effect/ai-*, @effect/opentelemetry, @effect/atom-*, @effect/vitest
- v3 to v4 migration guide available, dedicated Schema v4 migration guide
- v3 continues to receive bug fixes and security patches
- v4 will receive LTS designation upon stabilization
source_description: Effect 4.0 Beta release announcement from effect.website/blog

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: OpenAI Compat Package
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: OpenAI Compat Package for Minimal Schemas
Category: concept
Source: ai-openai-compat.md

Summary: Adds @effect/ai-openai-compat as a lighter alternative to @effect/ai-openai, providing OpenAI LanguageModel + embeddings with minimal, hand-written schemas instead of full codegen. Uses permissive schemas that accept unknown fields from the API.

Key Points:
- Same runtime behavior and error semantics as @effect/ai-openai for LanguageModel
- Minimal schemas cover only Responses + Embeddings endpoints
- Model id literals augmented with string fallback
- Unknown stream events ignored, not treated as errors
- HTTP calls implemented directly using HttpClient

Related Modules: @effect/ai-openai-compat, effect/unstable/ai
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/ai-openai-compat.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: AI Response HTTP Details
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: AI Response HTTP Request/Response Details
Category: concept
Source: ai-response-http-details.md

Summary: Adds HTTP request and response details to the AI SDK response protocol. Moves HttpRequestDetails and HttpResponseDetails schemas from AiError to Response, adding them to ResponseMetadataPart and FinishPart for success-path inspection.

Key Points:
- HTTP request details in ResponseMetadataPart (emitted at start of response)
- HTTP response details in FinishPart (emitted at end of response)
- Thread includeResponse: true through OpenAI and Anthropic provider clients
- Both streaming and non-streaming paths supported
- Header redaction preserved for sensitive headers

Related Modules: effect/unstable/ai/Response, effect/unstable/ai/AiError, @effect/ai-openai, @effect/ai-anthropic
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/ai-response-http-details.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: AI Tool Approval Protocol
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Tool Approval Protocol for Effect AI SDK
Category: concept
Source: ai-sdk-tool-approval-protocol.md

Summary: Adds human-in-the-loop tool approval to the Effect AI SDK. Tools can specify needsApproval (boolean or dynamic function), and the SDK emits tool-approval-request parts instead of executing. Users respond with tool-approval-response in tool messages.

Key Points:
- Two sources: framework-initiated (user-defined tools) and provider-initiated (MCP)
- needsApproval can be static boolean, dynamic function, or async Effect
- Pending approvals persist across conversation turns
- Denied calls sent as tool-result with execution-denied output
- Batch approvals supported (multiple responses in one message)
- No built-in timeout; users can wrap with Effect.timeout

Related Modules: effect/unstable/ai/Tool, effect/unstable/ai/Response, effect/unstable/ai/Prompt, effect/unstable/ai/LanguageModel
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/ai-sdk-tool-approval-protocol.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Atom Solid Bindings
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: @effect/atom-solid SolidJS bindings
Category: concept
Source: atom-solid-bindings.md

Summary: Adds @effect/atom-solid package providing SolidJS bindings for Effect Atom modules, mirroring @effect/atom-react feature set using Solid naming conventions (create* instead of use*).

Key Points:
- createAtomValue, createAtomSet, createAtomRefresh, createAtom, createAtomSuspense, etc.
- RegistryContext via Solid createContext, RegistryProvider component
- Suspense integration with Solid Suspense (client-side only)
- Excludes ScopedAtom and SSR-specific behavior
- Uses effect/unstable/reactivity for Atom types

Related Modules: @effect/atom-solid, effect/unstable/reactivity
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/atom-solid-bindings.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Remove Auto-Flattening
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Remove auto-flattening from all public APIs
Category: concept
Source: auto-flattening.md

Summary: Removes runtime isEffect auto-flattening from all public APIs. Callbacks must return Effect explicitly; plain values are no longer silently wrapped. Affects Effect.andThen, Effect.tap, Effect.onExit, Effect.when, Schedule, Stream, Sink, and Channel functions.

Key Points:
- Effect.andThen: no more conditional type overloads, requires Effect<B, E2, R2> return
- Effect.tap: removes NotFunction<X> overloads and { onlyEffect: true } option
- Effect.when: requires Effect<boolean>, not LazyArg<boolean>
- Schedule.addDelay, map, modifyDelay, reduce, unfold, while: require Effect return
- Stream.paginate, when: require Effect return
- Migration: wrap plain values in Effect.succeed() or Effect.sync()

Related Modules: effect/Effect, effect/Schedule, effect/Stream, effect/Sink, effect/Channel
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/auto-flattening.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Changesets v4 Beta Publishing
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Changesets v4 Beta Publishing
Category: concept
Source: changesets-v4-beta-publishing.md

Summary: Publishes all 24 public Effect packages under unified 4.0.0-beta.<n> pre-release versions using changesets fixed groups and pre-release mode. All packages share one version number.

Key Points:
- All 24 packages aligned to 3.0.0 before entering pre-release mode
- Fixed group ensures all packages bump together
- Pre-release mode appends -beta.<n> suffix
- Base version stays 4.0.0 regardless of subsequent patch/minor changesets
- Beta dist-tag used on npm, latest stays on stable v3
- Graduate to stable 4.0.0 with single command: npx changeset pre exit

Related Modules: All Effect ecosystem packages
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/changesets-v4-beta-publishing.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: CLI Completions Refactor
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Static CLI Shell Completions
Category: concept
Source: cli-completions-refactor.md

Summary: Replaces dynamic (re-invoke CLI) shell completions with static completion scripts generated from the command tree at build time. Supports Bash, Zsh, and Fish with comprehensive feature coverage including subcommands, flags, boolean negation, choice values, and file/directory hints.

Key Points:
- CommandDescriptor: shell-agnostic intermediate representation extracted from Command tree
- Three shell generators: Bash (complete builtin), Zsh (_arguments/_describe), Fish (complete -c)
- Supports --no-<flag> for boolean flags across all shells
- Used-flag filtering (Bash), exclusion groups (Zsh), __fish_contains_opt dedup (Fish)
- Dynamic completion system (--get-completions, COMP_* env vars) fully removed
- All phases completed with 17 descriptor tests and 41 generator tests

Related Modules: effect/unstable/cli
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/cli-completions-refactor.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: DevTools v3 Port
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: DevTools v3 Port (unstable)
Category: concept
Source: devtools-v3-port.md

Summary: Ports Effect v3 DevTools modules into v4 under packages/effect/src/unstable/devtools. Updates to v4 patterns (ServiceMap.Service, v4 Schema/Metric/Tracer APIs) with NDJSON wire protocol.

Key Points:
- DevToolsSchema: schemas for spans, metrics, ping/pong, requests/responses
- DevToolsClient: uses Socket, Queue for buffered requests, Ndjson duplex with Schema.toCodecJson
- DevToolsServer: accepts sockets via SocketServer.run, NDJSON duplex
- Uses Metric.snapshotUnsafe for metrics capture
- Default WebSocket URL ws://localhost:34437
- Importable from effect/unstable/devtools

Related Modules: effect/unstable/devtools, effect/unstable/socket, effect/unstable/encoding/Ndjson
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/devtools-v3-port.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Duration Negative Values
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Duration support for negative values
Category: concept
Source: duration-negative-values.md

Summary: Extends Duration type to represent negative values. Removes zero-clamp in make constructor, adds NegativeInfinity variant, and enables backward TestClock.adjust and natural DateTime subtraction via addDuration.

Key Points:
- DurationValue gains NegativeInfinity variant
- make constructor no longer clamps negative inputs to zero (NaN still becomes zero)
- New functions: isNegative, isPositive, abs, negate
- Signed infinity arithmetic follows standard rules
- TestClock.adjust with negative duration moves clock backward automatically
- DateTime.addDuration with negative duration naturally subtracts
- Schema.Duration codecs continue to reject negatives (no change)
- Consumer audit identifies HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW priority sites needing guards
- Implementation complete, all 5472 tests pass

Related Modules: effect/Duration, effect/testing/TestClock, effect/DateTime, effect/Schema
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/duration-negative-values.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Dynamic Tools for AI SDK
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Dynamic Tool Support for Effect AI SDK
Category: concept
Source: dynamic-tools.md

Summary: Adds Tool.dynamic() constructor supporting tools where schemas are not known at compile time (MCP tools, user configs, JSON Schema). Supports both Effect Schema mode (full type safety) and JSON Schema mode (handler receives unknown).

Key Points:
- Tool.dynamic(name, { parameters, success, failure, failureMode })
- parameters accepts Effect Schema OR raw JSON Schema
- JSON Schema mode: no validation, handler receives unknown
- Effect Schema mode: full decoding/validation like Tool.make
- success defaults to Schema.Unknown (vs Schema.Void for Tool.make)
- DynamicTypeId and isDynamic() type guard
- getJsonSchema() updated to handle both schema types
- No provider changes required

Related Modules: effect/unstable/ai/Tool, effect/unstable/ai/Toolkit
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/dynamic-tools.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Effect.ignore log option
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Effect.ignore log option + remove ignoreLogged
Category: concept
Source: effect-ignore-log.md

Summary: Removes Effect.ignoreLogged and extends Effect.ignore with optional logging configuration ({ log?: boolean | LogLevel }). Breaking change that simplifies the API.

Key Points:
- Effect.ignore(self, { log: true }) logs full Cause at current fiber log level
- Effect.ignore(self, { log: "Error" }) logs at specified LogLevel
- No options or log: false preserves current silent behavior
- Effect.ignoreLogged removed entirely
- Migration: Effect.ignoreLogged(effect) becomes Effect.ignore(effect, { log: true })

Related Modules: effect/Effect
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/effect-ignore-log.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Effect.ts JSDoc Improvements
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Effect.ts JSDoc Improvements
Category: concept
Source: effect-jsdoc-improvements.md

Summary: Documentation-only pass on Effect.ts to standardize category tags (Title Case), tighten summaries, update/add examples, and ensure @since tags. No runtime or API changes.

Key Points:
- Canonical category list defined (Caching, Creating Effects, Error Handling, Sequencing, etc.)
- One example per export, using Effect.gen and Console.log over console.log
- Output comments must match actual runtime output
- No Effect.run* calls unless showing output
- Preserve existing @since values

Related Modules: effect/Effect
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/effect-jsdoc-improvements.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Filter Refactor
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Filter Refactor - Box Pass Values and Accept Predicates
Category: concept
Source: filter-refactor.md

Summary: Refactors Filter.Filter to box both pass and fail return values (pass<B> | fail<X>), enabling every Filter-accepting API to also accept plain Predicate or Refinement via overloads. Major API unification merges *Map/*Filter variant pairs into single functions.

Key Points:
- Filter now returns pass<Pass> | fail<Fail> instead of unboxed pass values
- New constructors: Filter.pass(value), Filter.passVoid, Filter.isPass
- 22 APIs gain Predicate/Refinement overloads
- Phase 4: *Map variants merged into predicate names (Stream.filterMap -> Stream.filter, etc.)
- Phase 5: *Filter error-handling APIs renamed to *If (catchFilter -> catchIf, etc.)
- Array.filterMap subsumed by Array.filter with Filter overload
- Status: COMPLETE, all 5497 tests pass

Related Modules: effect/Filter, effect/Effect, effect/Stream, effect/Channel, effect/Sink, effect/Array
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/filter-refactor.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: HttpApi Client Middleware
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: HttpApi Client Middleware
Category: concept
Source: httpapi-client-middleware.md

Summary: Adds client-side middleware support to HttpApi, mirroring RpcMiddleware.layerClient pattern. Enables typed, composable middleware intercepting outgoing HTTP requests with access to endpoint/group metadata.

Key Points:
- HttpApiMiddlewareClient interface: receives { endpoint, group, request, next }
- HttpApiMiddleware.layerClient creates client-side middleware layers
- requiredForClient: true enforces middleware at type level
- clientError type parameter for typed client-side errors
- Middleware chain uses LIFO ordering (consistent with Rpc)
- Optional middleware (requiredForClient: false) skipped when not provided
- Registered under ${tag.key}/Client in service map

Related Modules: effect/unstable/httpapi/HttpApiMiddleware, effect/unstable/httpapi/HttpApiClient, effect/unstable/httpapi/HttpApiEndpoint
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/httpapi-client-middleware.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: HttpClientError Reason Pattern
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: HttpClientError Reason Pattern
Category: concept
Source: http-client-error-reason-pattern.md

Summary: Refactors HttpClientError to follow the reason pattern (like SocketError and AiError). Introduces per-reason error classes and a top-level HttpClientError wrapper with a reason field for Effect.catchReason ergonomics.

Key Points:
- Request reasons: TransportError, EncodeError, InvalidUrlError
- Response reasons: StatusCodeError, DecodeError, EmptyBodyError
- Top-level HttpClientError wraps reason, delegates message
- RequestError/ResponseError become type aliases
- Effect.catchReason("HttpClientError", "StatusCodeError", handler) for ergonomic handling
- Preserves existing message formatting and TypeId

Related Modules: effect/unstable/http/HttpClientError, effect/unstable/http/HttpClient
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/http-client-error-reason-pattern.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: HttpServerError Reason Pattern
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: HttpServerError Reason Pattern
Category: concept
Source: http-server-error-reason-pattern.md

Summary: Refactors HttpServerError to use the reason pattern. HttpServerError becomes a thin wrapper around a reason union of semantic reason classes with preserved response status mapping.

Key Points:
- Reason classes: RequestParseError (400), RouteNotFound (404), InternalError (500), ResponseError (500)
- Each implements Respondable.Respondable with status mapping
- Top-level HttpServerError wrapper delegates message and Respondable to reason
- ServeError excluded from isHttpServerError guard
- Effect.catchReason("HttpServerError", "RouteNotFound", handler)

Related Modules: effect/unstable/http/HttpServerError, effect/unstable/http/HttpRouter
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/http-server-error-reason-pattern.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Non-Strict JSON Schema for AI Tools
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Non-Strict JSON Schema Support for AI Tools
Category: concept
Source: non-strict-json-schema.md

Summary: Adds per-tool and per-provider control over the strict flag for tool calling. Uses Tool.Strict annotation with provider Config.strictJsonSchema as fallback.

Key Points:
- Tool.Strict = ServiceMap.Reference<boolean | undefined> annotation
- Resolution order: per-tool annotation -> provider config -> provider default
- OpenAI: default true; Anthropic: true when structured outputs supported
- Tool.getStrictMode(tool) helper to read annotation
- Anthropic gains strictJsonSchema on Config
- Only applies to tools, not Anthropic output_config.format

Related Modules: effect/unstable/ai/Tool, @effect/ai-openai, @effect/ai-anthropic
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/non-strict-json-schema.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: OpenRouter makeResponse Implementation
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: OpenRouter makeResponse Implementation
Category: concept
Source: openrouter-make-response.md

Summary: Implements the makeResponse function for OpenRouter provider, converting chat completion API responses into Effect AI SDK response parts. Status: DONE.

Key Points:
- No toolNameMapper needed (OpenRouter rejects provider-defined tools)
- Each reasoning detail gets its own reasoning part with metadata
- First tool call gets reasoningDetails attachment (prevents duplication)
- Gemini 3 workaround: override stop to tool_calls when encrypted reasoning + tool calls present
- Response conversion flow: response-metadata -> reasoning -> content -> tool-calls -> images -> finish
- Image handling: data URIs -> file parts, URLs -> source parts

Related Modules: @effect/ai-openrouter, effect/unstable/ai/Response
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/openrouter-make-response.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: ScopedAtom Port
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: ScopedAtom Module Port for @effect/atom-react
Category: concept
Source: scoped-atom-port.md

Summary: Ports the ScopedAtom module from legacy @effect-atom/atom-react into @effect/atom-react. Provides context-based scoping where each Provider instance owns its own Atom instance.

Key Points:
- ScopedAtom.make(f) creates a scoped atom with use() hook and Provider component
- use() throws when called outside Provider (strict undefined check)
- Each Provider creates Atom once via useRef
- Input-based factory receives value prop on first render only
- TypeId: "~@effect/atom-react/ScopedAtom"

Related Modules: @effect/atom-react, effect/unstable/reactivity/Atom
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/scoped-atom-port.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: SSE decodeSchema Refactor
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: SSE decodeSchema Refactoring
Category: concept
Source: sse-decodeSchema-refactor.md

Summary: Refactors SSE integration to use new Sse.decodeSchema API expecting a full event schema (event + id + data) instead of data-only schema. Old data-only version renamed to decodeDataSchema.

Key Points:
- Sse.EventSchema type = Schema.Top
- decodeSchema accepts full event schema, returns schema's Type directly
- Event schema composed at call site: Schema.Struct({ event: Schema.String, id: Schema.UndefinedOr(Schema.String), data: dataSchema })
- OpenAiClient updated to compose event schema and map .data
- OpenApiTransformer generates event schema composition inline

Related Modules: effect/unstable/encoding/Sse, @effect/ai-openai, @effect/openapi-generator
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/sse-decodeSchema-refactor.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Stream.ts JSDoc Improvements
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Stream.ts JSDoc Improvements
Category: concept
Source: stream-jsdoc-improvements.md

Summary: Documentation-only pass on Stream.ts for Effect 4.x. Standardizes category taxonomy, ensures one example per runtime export, and replaces Chunk usage with Array in examples.

Key Points:
- Canonical categories: Accumulation, Aggregation, Broadcast, Constructors, Deduplication, Destructors, etc.
- Stream now uses Array as collection type (not Chunk)
- Stream.runCollect already returns Array, no conversion needed
- Prefer Console.log over console.log with expected output comments
- Previously Known As notes where applicable

Related Modules: effect/Stream
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/stream-jsdoc-improvements.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Tool Approval Pre-Resolution Fix
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Tool Approval Pre-Resolution Bug Fix
Category: concept
Source: tool-approval-pre-resolution-fix.md

Summary: Fixes bug where tool call approvals are not resolved until AFTER the next LLM generation, causing LLM errors from unresolved tool calls. Status: COMPLETED.

Key Points:
- Bug: approval responses resolved after generateText call, LLM sees tool calls without results
- Fix: pre-resolve approved/denied tool calls BEFORE calling LLM
- New ToolkitRequiredError for when toolkit missing but approvals pending
- collectToolApprovals augmented with excludeResolved option and toolCall reference
- executeApprovedToolCalls helper runs approved tools with concurrency support
- createDenialResults generates execution-denied results
- Applied to both generateContent and streamContent paths

Related Modules: effect/unstable/ai/LanguageModel, effect/unstable/ai/AiError
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/tool-approval-pre-resolution-fix.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: Flexible Tool Parameters Schema
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: Flexible Tool Parameters Schema
Category: concept
Source: tool-parameters-flexible-schema.md

Summary: Relaxes Tool parameters from requiring Schema.Struct to accepting any Schema.Top, enabling Union, Array, and other schema types. Breaking change requiring existing code to wrap struct fields in Schema.Struct().

Key Points:
- Tool interface Config.parameters: Schema.Top (was Schema.Struct<Schema.Struct.Fields>)
- Enables Schema.Union parameters for tools like Anthropic CodeExecution
- Tool.make parameters now accepts any schema, defaults to Schema.Void
- ToolCallPart.params type relaxed accordingly
- getJsonSchemaFromSchema handles arbitrary schemas via Schema.toJsonSchemaDocument()
- No provider changes required
- Breaking: { name: Schema.String } must become Schema.Struct({ name: Schema.String })

Related Modules: effect/unstable/ai/Tool, effect/unstable/ai/Response
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/tool-parameters-flexible-schema.md

name: Effect v4 Design Spec: WorkerError Reason Pattern
episode_body:
Title: Design Spec: WorkerError Reason Pattern
Category: concept
Source: worker-error-reason-pattern.md

Summary: Refactors WorkerError to use the reason pattern. WorkerError becomes a thin wrapper around a reason union of WorkerSpawnError, WorkerSendError, WorkerReceiveError, and WorkerUnknownError.

Key Points:
- Four reason classes: WorkerSpawnError, WorkerSendError, WorkerReceiveError, WorkerUnknownError
- Each uses Schema.ErrorClass with required message and optional cause
- Top-level WorkerError wraps reason, delegates message
- isWorkerError guard targets only the wrapper
- Effect.catchReason usage enabled
- Updates Node, Bun, and Browser worker implementations

Related Modules: effect/unstable/workers/WorkerError, @effect/platform-node, @effect/platform-bun, @effect/platform-browser
source_description: Design spec from effect-smol/.specs/worker-error-reason-pattern.md

name: Effect v4 Import Map: Complete v4 import reference
episode_body:
Title: v4 Complete Import Map
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Complete import reference for Effect v4:

- Services: import { ServiceMap } from "effect" (NOT Context)
- Platform: import { FileSystem, Path, PlatformError } from "effect" (NOT @effect/platform)
- Platform layers: import { NodeFileSystem, NodePath } from "@effect/platform-node"
- Schema: import { Schema } from "effect"; Schema.decodeUnknownEffect(schema)(data) (NOT Schema.decode)
- Data structures: import { HashMap, HashSet } from "effect"; for (const [k, v] of map) (NOT HashMap.toIterable)
- Graph algorithms: import { Graph } from "effect"; Graph.topo(), Graph.isAcyclic(), Graph.dfs()
- Errors: import { Data } from "effect"; class MyError extends Data.TaggedError("MyError")<{ ... }> {}
- Testing: import { describe, expect, it } from "@effect/vitest"
- CLI: import { Command, Flag, Argument } from "effect/unstable/cli"

Affected Modules: All Effect v4 modules
source_description: Effect v4 complete import map from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Testing: Vitest patterns and conventions
episode_body:
Title: v4 Testing Patterns and Conventions
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Key testing patterns for Effect v4:

- ALWAYS use npx vitest run, NEVER bun test (Bun's runner breaks @effect/vitest)
- Type tests: bun run test:types (runs tstyche, files in tooling/*/dtslint/**/*.tst.*)
- layer() from @effect/vitest is essential for service-dependent tests
- Layer.provideMerge (not Layer.provide) when tests need dependency services directly
- it.effect("name", () => Effect.gen(function*() { ... })) for Effect tests
- CLI command tests: Command.runWith(cmd, { version })([args]) + TestConsole.logLines
- TestConsole from effect/testing captures Console.log/Console.error output
- Effect.exit(effect) to catch failures without v4's missing Effect.catchAll

Affected Modules: @effect/vitest, effect/testing
source_description: Effect v4 testing patterns from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Coding Style: Banned patterns and replacements
episode_body:
Title: v4 Coding Style: Banned patterns and their Effect replacements
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Banned patterns and their Effect v4 replacements:

- NEVER type assertions (as X) - only as const allowed; fix types properly
- NEVER native Map/Set - use MutableHashMap/MutableHashSet from effect
- NEVER non-null assertions (!) - use effect/Option (O.match, O.getOrElse, O.isSome)
- NEVER native Array methods (.push, .pop, .map, .filter, .length) - use effect/Array
- NEVER const arr: Array<T> = [] - use const arr = A.empty<T>()
- NEVER Object.keys - use Struct.keys from effect/Struct
- NEVER Array.from(iterable).sort() - use pipe(iterable, A.fromIterable, A.sort(Order.X))
- NEVER native Error - use DomainError or S.TaggedErrorClass
- NEVER raw JSON.parse/JSON.stringify - use Schema-based encode/decode
- NEVER null for optional values - use effect/Option (exception: 3rd-party APIs)
- NEVER native .match(regex) - use Str.match(regex) from effect/String
- Schema decoding inside Effects: ALWAYS use yield* S.decodeUnknown(Schema)(data)
- CLI output: Console.log/Console.error for user-facing, Effect.log for diagnostics

Affected Modules: All Effect v4 code
source_description: Effect v4 coding style conventions from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Schema: Annotation requirements
episode_body:
Title: v4 Schema Annotation Requirements
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Schema annotation requirements for Effect v4:

- ALWAYS annotate every schema with at minimum: identifier, title, description
- identifier format: "@scope/pkg/path/to/module/SchemaName"
- title: human-readable, spaced uppercase (e.g. "Package JSON")
- description: thoughtful, matches JSDoc, explains purpose and constraints
- Add examples when schema has a concrete decoded type (structs, branded types)
- Add toArbitrary if used in property-based tests (replaces v3 arbitrary)
- Add toFormatter if used in logging/display (replaces v3 pretty)
- Apply via .annotate({ identifier, title, description, examples }) on schema value
- For struct fields use .annotateKey({ description, messageMissingKey }) per-field

Affected Modules: effect/Schema
source_description: Effect v4 schema annotation requirements from MEMORY.md

name: Effect v4 Docgen: Requirements and conventions
episode_body:
Title: v4 Docgen Requirements
Category: migration
Source: MEMORY.md

Docgen requirements for Effect v4:

- Every individual named export needs its own /** @since 0.0.0 */ JSDoc
- Empty modules crash docgen - need at least one export (e.g., VERSION constant)
- Format: wrap destructured exports with per-item @since tags
- Run pnpm docgen to verify documentation generation

Affected Modules: All Effect v4 packages
source_description: Effect v4 docgen requirements from MEMORY.md
